channel.title: AI Explained
channel.description: Daily bite-sized episodes explaining AI concepts — from quantization to transformers. Narrated by Boti.
channel.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Autonomous Agents with Persistent Goals — Long-Running Agent Systems
item.description: How do AI agents work toward goals that span days, weeks, or months? This episode explores persistent goal architectures — from goal loops and prioritization to goal decomposition, context switching, and the supervisor-agent hierarchy. Learn what it takes to build agents that carry out missions, not just answer questions.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Consensus and Voting in Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: How do multiple AI agents agree on a single answer when they all see different evidence? This episode explores consensus mechanisms for multi-agent systems — from simple majority voting to weighted voting, structured debate, and adaptive protocols. Learn how to design agent teams that converge on better decisions together.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent communication protocols — Structured message passing between agents
item.description: How do AI agents talk to each other without getting confused? This episode explores agent communication protocols — from simple request-response to rich message formats with error handling, negotiation, and streaming. Learn about structured messages, correlation IDs, A2A, MCP, and why your agent team needs clear communication standards.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Specialization and role assignment in agent teams
item.description: Why does splitting work across specialized AI agents outperform a single generalist? This episode explores specialization and role assignment in multi-agent systems — from static roles and dynamic routing to emergent self-organization. Learn how role-based architectures improve reliability, simplify debugging, and scale complex agent workflows.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent delegation patterns — How agents hand off work to each other
item.description: How do AI agents pass work to each other effectively? This episode explores delegation patterns in multi-agent systems — from sequential pipelines to hierarchical supervisors and event-driven architectures. Learn how handoffs work, what context needs to transfer, and how to avoid common pitfalls like cascading failures and context dilution.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Autonomous agent loops — Self-directed task generation and execution
item.description: What happens when an AI agent generates its own tasks instead of waiting for instructions? This episode explores autonomous agent loops — how agents break down high-level goals, execute sub-tasks, and adapt on the fly. From AutoGPT to modern frameworks, learn how self-directed agents work and how to keep them safe.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Streaming and real-time agent interactions
item.description: Why wait for a full response when your agent can start acting mid-sentence? This episode covers streaming in AI agents — how token-by-token generation enables real-time collaboration, interleaved tool calls, and natural conversational flow. From server-sent events to voice-based real-time APIs, learn what makes streaming agents feel alive.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent orchestration — Sequential, hierarchical, and dynamic task graphs
item.description: How do you coordinate multiple AI agents to work together on complex tasks? This episode covers the three fundamental orchestration patterns: sequential pipelines for linear workflows, hierarchical supervisor-worker structures for flexible delegation, and dynamic self-organizing swarms for emergent coordination. Learn when to use each pattern and how real-world systems combine them.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent testing strategies — From unit tests to integration
item.description: Testing AI agents is fundamentally different from testing traditional software — agents are non-deterministic, behavior-driven systems. This episode covers the full testing pyramid: tool unit tests, prompt property tests, conversation flow tests, behavioral evaluation with LLM-as-judge, adversarial testing, and integration testing against real APIs.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agentic RAG — When agents meet retrieval
item.description: Classic RAG retrieves documents once and hopes for the best. Agentic RAG gives the agent tools to iteratively search, refine queries, multi-hop across sources, and self-verify its answers. This episode covers query refinement loops, multi-hop retrieval, sub-question decomposition, and the production challenges of building RAG systems that think.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Building Reliable Agents — Retries, Fallbacks, Circuit Breakers, Timeouts
item.description: AI agents in production face failures constantly — APIs go down, networks timeout, models return garbled output. This episode covers the four essential reliability patterns every agent needs: retries with exponential backoff, fallback strategies, circuit breakers to prevent cascading failures, and timeouts to keep agents responsive.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: State Management for Agents — Persistence, Memory, and Context
item.description: AI agents need to track where they are, what they've done, and where they're going across multiple steps, conversations, and sessions. This episode covers state machines, checkpointing, hot vs cold state, persistent storage, and distributed state in multi-agent systems.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Tool Design Best Practices — Writing Tools Agents Can Actually Use
item.description: Your AI agent is only as capable as the tools you give it. This episode covers the essential principles of designing tools that language models can actually use effectively — from clear descriptions and flat parameters to robust error messages and proper granularity.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent Architecture Patterns — Supervisor, Swarm, Router, Event-Driven
item.description: An exploration of the four major agent architecture patterns used in production AI systems. Learn how supervisor hierarchy, decentralized swarms, lightweight routers, and event-driven automation each solve different coordination problems, and how to choose the right pattern for your use case.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Building an Agent From Scratch — The Perceive-Think-Act Loop
item.description: A deep dive into the fundamental perceive-think-act loop that powers every AI agent. Learn how perception, reasoning, and action work together in a continuous cycle, how to handle errors and termination conditions, and how a simple three-step pattern gives agents the power to tackle complex multi-step tasks autonomously.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent Security — Prompt Injection, Tool Access Control, and Sandboxing
item.description: A practical guide to AI agent security covering prompt injection attacks (direct and indirect), tool access control with least privilege and role-based permissions, output validation, and sandboxing strategies from Docker containers to virtual machines. Learn how to build agent systems that are secure by default.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop — Designing Safe Agentic Workflows
item.description: A practical guide to human-in-the-loop design for AI agents — from approval gates and escalation paths to the three levels of human involvement. Learn how to balance agent autonomy with human oversight to build safe, effective agent systems.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Open-Source Agent Frameworks Compared — LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Smolagents
item.description: A practical comparison of four open-source agent frameworks — LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, and Smolagents — each taking a different approach to building AI agents. From flexible Swiss Army knives to code-first minimalists, we break down which framework fits which use case.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: A2A — Google's Agent-to-Agent Protocol
item.description: How Google's Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A) enables AI agents built by different teams using different frameworks to discover, communicate, and collaborate with each other. We explore agent cards, task-oriented communication, and how A2A complements MCP to create connected agent ecosystems.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Coding Agents — SWE-bench and Autonomous Software Engineering
item.description: How AI agents are learning to write, debug, and test code autonomously. We cover the SWE-bench benchmark, the agentic strategies that drive success rates from 2% to over 40%, and what this means for the future of software engineering.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Evaluation of Agent Systems — Measuring What Matters
item.description: Why evaluating AI agents is harder than evaluating regular language models. We cover end-to-end success metrics, progressive evaluation, benchmark suites like AgentBench and SWE-bench, trajectory-level evaluation, safety testing, and efficiency metrics for production agent systems.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Safety and Guardrails for AI Agents
item.description: Why AI agents need safety measures beyond what regular language models require. We cover input guardrails, tool-level constraints, output validation, human-in-the-loop approval, and why layered guardrails are essential for building reliable agentic systems.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: MCP (Model Context Protocol) — Standardizing Agent Tools
item.description: How the Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardizes tool access for AI agents. We cover MCP servers, clients, the transport layer, and why this USB-C-like standard is transforming how agents connect to external tools and services.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Planning and Decomposition — How Agents Break Down Tasks
item.description: How AI agents plan and decompose complex goals into actionable steps. We cover implicit planning, explicit planning, hierarchical approaches, Chain of Thought, Tree of Thoughts, and the planner-verifier pattern that makes multi-step agent tasks reliable.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent Observability — Tracing and Debugging AI Agents
item.description: How to see what your AI agent is actually doing — tracing every thought, tool call, and decision. We cover the fundamentals of observability for probabilistic agent systems, key metrics to track, common debugging patterns like infinite loops and hallucinated tool arguments, and the tools available for agent monitoring.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Beam Search vs Greedy Decoding — How AI Picks Words
item.description: How language models decide which word to generate next. We compare greedy decoding — picking the single most likely next word — against beam search, which explores multiple possible sequences in parallel. Learn when each strategy works best and why the choice matters for translation, chat, and creative writing.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Positional Encoding — How AI Knows Word Order
item.description: How transformers understand word order through positional encoding. We cover sinusoidal encodings, learned embeddings, and Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE), explaining why these techniques are essential for modern language models like Llama, GPT, and Claude.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: KV Cache — How AI Remembers Within a Conversation
item.description: The KV cache is the silent workhorse behind every AI chat application. We explore how caching key-value vectors in the attention mechanism makes long conversations possible, the memory trade-offs involved, and cutting-edge optimization techniques.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: ReAct — The Pattern That Makes AI Agents Actually Work
item.description: The ReAct pattern — Reasoning plus Acting — is the foundation of modern AI agents. We explore the 2022 paper by Yao et al., how the think-act-observe loop works, and why combining reasoning with tool use beats either approach alone.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Tool Use and Function Calling — Giving AI the Power to Act
item.description: How language models call external tools and APIs. We cover function calling schemas, the separation of suggestion and execution, tool selection at scale, error handling, and why tool composition is the frontier of agent capability.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Multi-Agent Systems — Orchestrating Multiple AI Agents
item.description: What happens when you have a whole team of specialized AI agents working together? We explore orchestration patterns — supervisor, peer-to-peer, and debate — and when multi-agent systems actually outperform a single agent.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agent Memory — How Agents Remember and Forget
item.description: Memory systems for AI agents: episodic, semantic, procedural, and working memory. We cover RAG-based retrieval, memory management strategies, session vs persistent memory, and how to design an agent that remembers what matters.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Structured Output — JSON Mode and Constrained Generation
item.description: How to make AI models return cleanly formatted JSON instead of messy prose. We cover JSON mode, grammar-based constrained generation, tool calling, and when to use each approach in production agent systems.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Speculative Decoding — Making Inference Faster
item.description: How a small draft model can speed up large language model inference by 2-3x without changing the output at all. We explore the elegant trick of letting a cheap model guess and an expensive model verify.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Multimodal Models — AI That Sees, Hears, and Reads
item.description: How modern AI systems process images, audio, and text together. We explore early fusion, cross-attention, contrastive learning (CLIP), and why multimodality is the future of AI.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Open Source vs Closed Models — The AI Access Debate
item.description: The battle between open and closed AI models is reshaping the industry. We explore what open source really means in AI, why Meta's Llama and Mistral are closing the gap with GPT and Claude, and how to choose between them for your own projects.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Benchmarks — How We Measure AI Intelligence
item.description: What do MMLU, GPQA, and SWE-bench actually test? We break down how AI benchmarks work, why they matter, and why you shouldn't trust any single score.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Distillation — Shrinking Smart Models into Small Ones
item.description: How knowledge distillation transfers the intelligence of massive AI models into smaller, faster ones you can run on a phone. We cover soft targets, temperature scaling, teacher-student training, and real-world examples like DistilBERT.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agents and Tool Use — AI That Can Take Actions
item.description: Language models can generate impressive text, but agents take it further — they can actually do things. This episode explains how tool use and function calling turn language models into systems that perceive, act, and adapt in the real world.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Chain of Thought — Teaching AI to Reason Step by Step
item.description: How a simple prompting trick — asking AI to show its work — unlocked reasoning capabilities in large language models, from Jason Wei's 2022 paper to modern reasoning models like o1.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Hallucinations — Why AI Makes Things Up
item.description: Why do AI models confidently state things that are completely false? We explore the mechanics behind AI hallucinations — from statistical pattern matching to training data gaps — and what techniques like RAG, RLHF, and chain-of-thought are doing to fight back.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: GPUs vs CPUs — Why AI Needs Special Hardware
item.description: Why does AI training require GPUs instead of regular CPUs? We explore parallel computing, Tensor Cores, memory bandwidth, and how the gaming industry accidentally built the perfect AI chip.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Backpropagation — How AI Learns from Mistakes
item.description: The algorithm that makes neural network training possible. We break down how backpropagation uses the chain rule to propagate errors backwards through a network, computing gradients efficiently so every weight gets nudged in the right direction.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Neural Network Basics — Layers, Weights, and Activations
item.description: What actually is a neural network? We break it down into its three core building blocks — layers that give structure, weights that store learned knowledge, and activation functions that make deep learning possible.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Diffusion Models — How AI Generates Images
item.description: How do tools like Stable Diffusion and DALL-E create images from text? We break down the elegant idea behind diffusion models: learn to add noise, then run the process in reverse. From denoising basics to latent diffusion and text conditioning, this episode explains the technology powering AI image generation.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Mixture of Experts — Why Some Models Are Huge but Fast
item.description: How Mixture of Experts lets AI models have billions of parameters but only use a fraction at a time — giving you the intelligence of a huge model with the speed of a small one.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: RLHF — How AI Learns Human Preferences
item.description: How do you teach AI what 'good' means when you can't write it as a formula? RLHF lets humans show their preferences through comparisons, training a reward model that guides language models toward more helpful, safer, and more natural responses. We break down the full pipeline — from human rankings to reward models to reinforcement learning — and explore why this technique powers ChatGPT, Claude, and the modern AI assistants you use every day.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Prompt Engineering — Talking to AI Effectively
item.description: The art and science of crafting effective prompts for AI models. We cover role assignment, few-shot and zero-shot prompting, chain-of-thought reasoning, context engineering, and practical tips for getting better results from any language model.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Context Windows — How Much AI Can Remember at Once
item.description: What is a context window, and why does it matter? We explore the fixed-size memory that limits how much text an AI model can consider at once — from tiny 1K-token windows to today's two-million-token giants — and why bigger isn't always better.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: LoRA and Adapters — Cheap Fine-Tuning for Everyone
item.description: How LoRA lets you customize massive AI models by training just a tiny fraction of their parameters. The clever math behind low-rank adaptation, and why it democratized fine-tuning.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Temperature and Sampling — Why AI Responses Vary
item.description: Ever wonder why AI gives different answers to the same question? This episode explores temperature, top-k, top-p sampling, and the math behind how language models choose their words — from greedy decoding to creative chaos.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Embeddings — How AI Understands Meaning as Numbers
item.description: What are embeddings and why do they matter? We explore how AI converts words, sentences, and documents into lists of numbers that capture meaning — enabling semantic search, recommendations, and RAG. From Word2Vec to modern transformers, discover the bridge between human meaning and machine math.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) — Giving AI a Reference Library
item.description: How RAG lets AI models look things up instead of guessing. We cover vector databases, semantic search, the three-step pipeline of indexing, retrieval, and generation — and why this technique is behind most reliable AI assistants today.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Fine-Tuning vs Pre-Training — Teaching Old Models New Tricks
item.description: What's the difference between pre-training and fine-tuning a language model? We break down the two-stage process behind modern AI: the expensive foundation-building phase and the affordable customization step that makes models actually useful.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Attention Mechanism — How Models Focus on What Matters
item.description: From the cocktail party effect to the Transformer revolution: how attention lets AI models decide what's important in a sea of information. We break down queries, keys, values, multi-head attention, and why this single idea powers every major language model today.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Transformers — The architecture behind modern AI
item.description: How does the architecture powering GPT, Claude, and every major AI model actually work? We break down the transformer — from the limitations of older recurrent networks, to the self-attention mechanism that lets models process entire sentences at once. Queries, keys, values, multi-head attention, and why this 2017 paper changed everything.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Tokenization — How AI reads text
item.description: How do language models convert human text into numbers they can process? We explore tokenization — from simple character mapping to sophisticated subword algorithms like BPE. Why the word 'tokenization' might be split into multiple pieces, and why this invisible step shapes everything about AI text processing.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Quantization — How AI Models Shrink to Fit Your Laptop
item.description: What is quantization? How does it let you run massive AI models on a laptop with 8GB of RAM? We break down the math, the formats (GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ), and why neural networks are surprisingly tolerant of rounding.
item.itunes:author: Boti
